tokens: Al, what I think we can do is walk down here about 300 feet (east along the rim) and walk straight down that slope to it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It doesn't look so bad from here, does it, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, will that direction of your travel be to the northeast direction?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: No, what we do is go directly east and then walk directly sort of north, you know, curving right around and down to it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're going directly east and then you'll be curving around going up north towards the Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You get kind of an optical illusion depending on where you're standing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You'd better put that one in.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I wanted to put it back on here, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't think it makes any difference (whether or not the magazine 49 that they are taking off the LMP camera has a
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Boy, these cameras got dirty, didn't they?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, a reminder on that film pack.
Cycle one frame before you start.

tokens: We cycled one before we took it off, too.
I think we're in good shape, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Good thing we've practiced this a few times.
Why don't you let me hold it for you?

tokens: Here, I know what you need to do.
Pull this out of the way.
The (magazine) lock is what's driving me buggy.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute, wait a minute.
Okay, keep the (CDR camera?) trigger pulled.
Okay, let me put it on (Al's RCU).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That lens is good and clean, too.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hope we didn't get anything on the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Talking a bit louder, suggesting Pete has moved off ahead of him) Yeah.
I think a strap on you or something like that - (on) each of you - so if you want to lean over and pick up a rock, the other guy just holds the strap, you lean over and pick up the rock and go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I was going to say as long as we're going - (Hearing Gibson) What?
A few minutes ago, Pete wanted to pick up a rock, so I held onto that strap of the Surveyor bag and he leaned right over and picked it up and I helped him get back up.
It's not that you're heavy or anything, it's the fact that you have such poor balance.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Before you go much further, could you stop and have a little break there before you proceed on down the slope?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We are just going to move to the area, where we could stop and case the joint.
Al, grab a shot of that beaded glass there and we'll bag it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's better than the Hope diamond.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Better take that...(Pause)...area over in here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've got too many of them (that is, too many glass samples compared with rocks).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, we did get a lot of these?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We could get the rock with it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, here's some rocks right here.
(Pause) You know, we keep collecting a lot of the same type of rock, because there just doesn't seem to be any other kinds around.
(Pause) I haven't seen any microbreccia the whole day.
All I have seen is some basalt; I've seen nothing that looked vesicular at all, except on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's not at all like Neil's rocks.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, we show you're 2 plus 23 into the EVA and, based on a 4-hour EVA, you would be leaving the Surveyor at 2 plus 50.
But don't rush; we'd like to make sure you get a good rest before you go into it (the crater).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Why don't you give me a rock or two, Pete?
And I will stick in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Ed) We just made a sample of a glass bead and some local rock on the south edge of the Surveyor Crater, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) brings back all of our training.
I'm trying to remember who the guy was that kept saying "whatever you do, don't get dust on the gnomon." (Hearty laughter from both) Okay.
We are going to jog on here for a little bit, Houston, and get a little bit closer to the Surveyor and look her over.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, that's...coming in from the south; looks like a good way, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you what I'm going to do, Al.
I am just gonna lope right around here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you stay at this level, you'll end up at Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Look at the (Surveyor) scoop sticking out.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete and Al, could you give us a comment on how far you're sinking in?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Pete and Al, could you give us a comment on how far you're sinking in?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Not sinking in very far at all.
And I'm down in the crater about the same distance down that Surveyor is.
I'm just going around it radially (means "circumferentially").
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, I would say that...I think Houston is just as concerned about us getting down in this crater.
We been thinking about it, too, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, don't worry about it, Houston, because, really, it's no strain; I'm 200 feet away from it; I'm at the same level; the ground is firm; and I can go right back up the way I came down with no strain at all.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's just exactly the way we thought it was going to be when we were talking about it.
I don't think there'll be any sweat about it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, I'll tell you what let's do.
Get right over here, and we'll park all of our gear, take ourselves a little rest, go over your photo plan, and then we'll have at it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I will tell you what, why don't you get a photograph of it right now?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Stop and do it right here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I'm) trying to see which way it landed.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, Pete and Al, when you are looking at it there, would you also try to determine whether there is any effect from the dust during the (LM) descent?

transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I actually flew around it; however, I probably passed closer to it (on the north side of the crater) than I am parked to it right now.

transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Any dust you had on the edge would never go down to this crater.

tokens: What happened to your volume, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We read you both loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm going to mosey up here just a little, Pete, without any tools, which makes it pretty easy.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's leave the whole...(Very brief pause while he reconsiders) I tell you what; let's take the tool carrier with us!...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...I think we can go right up the other rim and around to...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...that big blocky baby (Block Crater) there and...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Where all that rock is just...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You know, I could have landed the LM in the bottom of that (Surveyor) crater.

tokens: Would you carry the hand-tool carrier down there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want me take some pictures up here around it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can see which way it came in.

tokens: It's going to make a good shot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (According to the checklist) we're not supposed to take pictures of that leg.

(Pause; breathing fairly heavily as he maneuvers to take pictures).
You know, this one (meaning Surveyor III)'s brown and I don't remember ours being brown there at the Cape.

tokens: (It's) kind of a light tan or maybe that's the way it's changed color.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks a light tan now.
(Pause) Hey, this crater isn't as steep as we thought, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to get dust on her (the Surveyor).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) I'll stop here, and this will be my last picture.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, the equipment bays were white on the side, and the scoop itself was a light blue.

tokens: Well, it's kind of a...Well, we'll get down there and get closer inspection.

For example, all of the struts and the like?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The equipment bays and the primary structure was all painted with a white paint.

tokens: Sort of turned tan or something.
We'll have to look at it more closely.

tokens: The Sun's cooked that paint brown.

tokens: On the slopes here, it's just a little bit softer.

tokens: I don't think it (the soil)'s any deeper.

tokens: Then we'll take the rest down here where we can see it better.

tokens: Let's just make sure we don't get any dirt down there on it.

tokens: (Pause) Hey, you can see...Look at there where it dug those scoops.

tokens: Wonder if that was from us?

tokens: I think I'll take a picture of that.

tokens: Boy, this thing (the 70 mm camera) is dusty.

tokens: Why don't you go ahead and put this together?

tokens: Well, I was thinking of that earlier and decided that, since this one broke, we might have to put that one on.

tokens: (Still unsuccessful) (Garbled, but originally transcribed as "Makes you kind of mad".)

tokens: Let me look down there just once more.

tokens: I'm holding it (the HTC) with this post.

tokens: Maybe I'd better back up a little.

tokens: Hey, this cuff checklist sure helps do the job.

tokens: Disturbed surface by the footpad-2 area.

tokens: (Pause) Get a little bit to the east of...Looks pretty good.

tokens: In fact that green seems to have had less change than most of the rest.

tokens: I was checking something else, real quick.

tokens: Now be careful about the glass.

tokens: Let me look and see what it looks like.
Houston, not a bit of this glass is cracked.
One little piece down here looks like it no longer reflects, but other than that, it's in perfect condition.
A little warped - (some) segments warped - but, other than that, it looks pretty much the same (as the mock-up they used in training).
The thing that's the most amazing to me is how it's turned so brown!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, if I wipe it with my...(Responding to Gibson) I'm going to wipe it, but not with my glove.
I'm going to wipe it with this little cloth that protects my wrist ring.
It doesn't have anything structurally associated with it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It wipes off just like you'd expect dust to wipe off glass.

tokens: Let me wipe a couple of spots, though it might...It's going to be tough to show this, Pete.

tokens: They're better than no pictures at all.

tokens: Now that's pretty much in the shadow.
I'm going to open it (the f-stop) up a little bit.

tokens: It's in the shadow of the landing radar or the instrument box.

tokens: I think you ought to photo that scoop (means "footpad") there, the way it dug in.

tokens: There's no way that thing can slide down the hill on us, the way it's dug in.

tokens: Okay, now let me get that footpad.

tokens: And that's going to be in f/8, probably.

(To Houston, louder than he's been talking to Pete or, for that matter, to Houston, lately) That aft honeycomb shock absorber struck the dirt and looks like it took some of the shock.
Other than that, the front one didn't appear to do that.
Sure isn't going to slide down the hill though, that's for sure.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Back up 15 feet and take it...

tokens: Going to be a tough shot, because it's in the Sun, but I'll give it a go.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm trying to shoot the top of it.
Go ahead (with the next item on the checklist).

tokens: Boy, those solar arrays are not blue anymore; they're black.

tokens: (Pause) Okay, move around to the front.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right, got it in sight.
It dug in real well, too.

tokens: There's some...Hey, let me get the

tokens: Now I photograph the trenches, right?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, this is so much easier working around than in one g in our practice; it's unbelievable.

tokens: "Photo the scoop trenches at 5 feet, f/8 in stereo."
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Watch it now; you're going to get dust on them.

tokens: Camera (film advance mechanism)'s moving all the time, so I guess we're getting it.

tokens: No, it's brown because it's looking at brown, isn't it?

tokens: Maybe it's got some coating on it.
Why don't you stay right there, and I'll come in and wipe it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Come on in and wipe it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I can) see the mechanical components down inside it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get a shot of that; that will be a good...Let me see if I'm set right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get over here, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You don't get a chance like this every day.
Shoot up the extras; we've got lots of film.

tokens: Why don't you get yourself in the photo, too?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Back up just a little, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Chuckling) You got a calibrated eye, huh?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Trouble with that camera is, if it's not right on the money, it's out of focus.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The Surveyor is firmly planted here; that's no problem.
We're ready to start getting the
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Pete, you want to do something for me first?

tokens: (Garbled; Pause) Hang that (70 mm camera) back on the front (of Al's

tokens: (Pause) Hey, this is easier in one-sixth g.

tokens: We read you loud and clear.

tokens: No, it goes around the camera.

tokens: Okay, one shiny tube coming up.

tokens: We're going to have to pick another tube.

tokens: Okay, that'll be a good shot.

tokens: Come on, boy; we got to move!

tokens: (Pause) Thanks to Joe Roberts, this bag's going to do the job, I think.

tokens: Well, it's not...Get a little open here.

tokens: Hey, I got a shiny tube for you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, two more tubes on that TV camera and that baby's ours.

transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get a grip on it (probably the TV camera) now.

tokens: (Garbled under Pete laughter) we got her!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here, let me lay it on you man!

tokens: Let's get the (Surveyor) scoop while we're at it (and take it back to Earth).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think I can hold that with one hand and open the bag.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, I've got to zip it up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Responding to Gibson) It's in the bag!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let me get around to the other side of you.

This was one of the hard parts.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Want me to bend over a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) One more pull and I've got it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) (Looking at his gauge) I've got lots of oxygen.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, let me put the covers down on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Before you get that thing buried too deep, how about letting me cut this (Surveyor) scoop off?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, okay; (but) the scoop goes right in here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, you've got a place for it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You didn't think you were going to leave without a scoop, did you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's what they said, but...Let me hold it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute; let me help.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: They wanted it ...No, they wanted it just aft of that joint.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, they wanted that joint if they could get it.
(Pause) No, you have to ro(tate)...No, you're okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's even got dirt in it!
(Pete laughs) Bring back some of the original dirt!
Got an extra sample for you, Houston.
The scoop's got dirt in it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Say, when you move out from here...Well, first of all, we show you're 3 hours into the EVA; and you're about 10 minutes behind nominal traverse we have figured out for a 4-hour EVA.
However, your PLSS consumables are holding out real well, so we suggest you go on with the nominal traverse.
We may want you to cut down to perhaps just one sample at Blocky (sic) Crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, that's what I wanted to do is go to Blocky Crater if you agree...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We thought this thing had changed color, but I think it's just dust.
We rubbed into that battery, and it's good and shiny again.
Let me get a shot on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think that's what...Maybe this thing's just collecting all this red dust.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, before you leave there, also, would you get some of those geosamples which we've discussed, as well as some of the loose soil from that area?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Let me give the Surveyor tool (the bolt cutters) a heave.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We don't need it for anything, do we?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, we don't need this Surveyor tool anymore, do we?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you've got the TV back at the LM already cut off, then there is no more need for it.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Al, have I got my scoop on?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me go get the sample bags.
(Pause) Hey, that's a good one (that is, a good rock).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't think the (Surveyor) TV could see that one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm trying to remember where that...(Garbled) some platey ones?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I see one up there, right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where's the one that had the lines in it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think it's right up here by this crater.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Before you leave the area of the Surveyor, would you take a look back at the Surveyor and see whether the direction of the sunlight has any effect on the colors which you see?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Let me get this one in the bag, too.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Didn't know you had it, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Answering Gibson) No, it's light brown wherever you look at it, up-Sun, down-Sun, or cross-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: But, strangely enough, that light brown rubs off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's funny, because the dirt here is not brown.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look, is that the rock right there?
(To Ed) You know, these rocks, as they showed in the Surveyor pictures, all have this soil built up around them.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, they all have fillets around them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm trying to remember where...I can't orient myself to the pictures, can you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Should we grab this one right here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's a good rock right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't know whether I can get that or not.
Okay, let's head for Blocky Crater.
Pick up a couple of more of these in route.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, let's get that brick-looking one over there (12064).
I think that's one of them they saw there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Up the hill a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, we have comment on what you just said about brushing up against the battery case.
Would you make sure that anything which you picked up against that battery case you clean off your EMU?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: There wasn't any signs of KOH anywhere....
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Pete) Right here's the one; the square one, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think it is; that is, for here.
